display.purpose: One hero statement and major section arguments.

body.purpose: Plainspoken explanations capped at 68 characters per line.

code.purpose: Commands, Skill names, and machine output only.

motion.name: state
motion.purpose: Hover, focus, copy confirmation, and language changes.

motion.name: entrance
motion.purpose: One orchestrated first-load reveal, disabled for reduced motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single dominant action within a section.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: A quieter action next to the section primary.

components.name: Command Block
components.css: .ds-command{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:16px;background:#f7f7fa;color:#17161b;border-radius:12px;padding:16px 18px;font:500 14px/1.5 SFMono-Regular,Consolas,monospace}.ds-command code{flex:1}.ds-command button{background:none;border:0;color:#563aa1;font:650 13px/1.2 Avenir Next,Segoe UI,sans-serif}.ds-command button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #8d68ec;outline-offset:3px}

components.name: Language Control
components.description: Switches English and Chinese without reloading the page.

components.name: Workflow Step
components.description: One owned step in the engineering sequence, placed on a shared rail.
components.html:
Find the owner

Trace the symptom to the boundary that owns the behavior.

narrative.overview: A precise workshop tool shown on a clean bench. The hand-drawn loom carries the human character while open bands, black rules, and restrained spot colors keep the interface exact and inspectable.
keyCharacteristics: True white canvas with near-black structural ink
Open layouts connected by rules and thread paths
Spot color used only for meaning

rules.name: The Spot Color Rule
rules.body: Accent colors explain state or sequence and occupy less than 12% of a viewport.

rules.name: The Literal Mono Rule
rules.body: Monospace is reserved for commands, Skill names, and machine output.

rules.name: The Bench Rule
rules.body: Content rests on the page; it does not float in glass or shadow stacks.

dos: Keep the installation action visible in the first viewport.
Use the cat-at-the-loom image as the single signature illustration.
State limits with the same visual weight as capabilities.
donts: Do not use generic purple-gradient SaaS pages, glass panels, or soft glowing blobs.
Do not build repetitive feature-card grids or giant rounded wrappers.
Do not use terminal cosplay, fake metrics, inflated AI claims, or dense audit terminology in the first viewport.
